items.text: Dana Whitfield; uses she/her or he/him pronouns (partner Sam uses 'he'); lives in Portland

Product manager at a healthcare data company; mid-quarter as of 2025-02-03, preparing for roadmap review on Thursday 2025-02-06; facing prioritization decision between interoperability work and analytics dashboard
Sam (partner); together six years as of 2025-02-03; described as 'great' support during difficult time
Marcus (brother); lives in Denver; described as close but haven't seen each other in over a year as of 2025-02-03, communication has drifted to phone calls only

supersededBy.text: Marcus (brother); lives in Denver with partner Elena; daughter Nina born 2025-03-06; Marcus called Dana from hospital at 2am to share the news ('voice shaking, no agenda'); described as close but hadn't seen each other in over a year as of 2025-02-03, though communication quality has improved as of 2025-03-09

items.text: Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year (2025); stated as 'real intention' on 2025-02-03

supersededBy.text: Plans to visit brother Marcus in Denver before summer 2025 to meet niece Nina while she's still tiny; Marcus offered guest room; Dana described this as 'obligation, the good kind' and 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' (upgraded from intention as of 2025-03-09)

items.text: Lost mother during winter before 2025-02-03; described as 'heavy winter' and 'it colors everything'; did not want to discuss details in session on 2025-02-03
Priya (closest friend from grad school); ceramicist and freelance designer; as of 2025-02-12, signed up together for Tuesday night pottery class — first standing calendar commitment in years

Started restoring mother's wooden sailboat (22-foot sailboat named Dorothy Jean, after Dana's grandmother) on 2025-02-10; boat stored under tarp in Priya's dad's barn near Scappoose; mother taught Dana to sail on this boat; restoration is intentionally slow with no deadline

On 2025-02-10, pulled tarp off mother's sailboat Dorothy Jean and sanded one plank — described as 'the first right thing in months'; said 'working with my hands on something of hers felt like the first right thing in months' and 'I'm not ready to talk about the rest of it yet' (2025-02-12)
Attends Tuesday night pottery class with Priya starting 2025-02 (new standing commitment as of 2025-02-12)

[as of 2025-02-24] Training for Lakefront Marathon in October 2025; four runs per week plus long run every Saturday morning; ran eight miles on Saturday 2025-02-22; has training plan with gradual weekly mileage increase and cutback weeks every fourth week; casual runner for years with 5Ks and one half marathon about four years prior
On 2025-02-24, said of marathon training: 'It's the first time since mom that I've been excited about something in the future instead of dreading it' (2025-02-24)
Elena: Elena (Marcus's partner); gave birth to daughter Nina on 2025-03-06
Nina: Nina (niece, Marcus and Elena's daughter); born 2025-03-06; Dana saw photo of her in knit hat and cried at desk

Marcus (brother); lives in Denver with partner Elena; daughter Nina born 2025-03-06; Marcus called Dana from hospital at 2am to share the news ('voice shaking, no agenda'); described as close but hadn't seen each other in over a year as of 2025-02-03, though communication quality has improved as of 2025-03-09

Plans to visit brother Marcus in Denver before summer 2025 to meet niece Nina while she's still tiny; Marcus offered guest room; Dana described this as 'obligation, the good kind' and 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' (upgraded from intention as of 2025-03-09)

On 2025-03-09, upon learning of niece Nina's birth, said: 'Marcus called me from the hospital hallway at like 2am their time just to say "she's here." We haven't talked like that — voice shaking, no agenda — in years. Denver suddenly feels way too far away.'

On March 14, 2025, scattered mother's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge; went alone; said 'Beacon Rock was ours — she used to take me there when I was a kid, and on the way up she taught me to read cloud shapes. Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water. I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' Picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down, now on windowsill above kitchen sink. Sam had soup waiting when Dana got home; slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)
[as of 2025-03-18] In cutback week of marathon training as of 2025-03-18
